message:
feat(fwc): add structured tool inventory provenance to export-tools and MCP surfaces

Replaces hardcoded mode strings in export-tools and MCP server responses
with structured provenance metadata derived from the actual tool source,
enabling agents and operators to programmatically verify whether tool
information is authoritative (live-host) or advisory (offline-artifact).

main.rs (+230 lines):
- tool_inventory_mode_label(): maps ToolInventorySource to human-readable
  mode string
- tool_inventory_surface_state(): maps source to McpSurfaceState
- exported_tool_name(): extracts the exported name from an operation
  across all formats (MCP, OpenAI, raw)
- tool_inventory_provenance(): builds Vec<ExportedToolProvenance> for a
  batch of operations with per-tool source tracking
- attach_tool_inventory_provenance(): injects mode, surface_state,
  provenance, and tool_provenance into JSON payloads
- export_tools_dispatch/export_tools_dispatch_host: replaced flat
  "mode" strings with attach_tool_inventory_provenance() calls
- host_mcp_tool_definitions(): now attaches annotations and provenance
  to each McpToolDefinition
- New test helper assert_tool_inventory_provenance() and updated test
  assertions for both offline and live-host export paths

serve_mcp.rs (+235 lines):
- McpToolDefinition: new optional annotations and provenance fields
  with builder methods with_annotations() and with_provenance()
- handle_tools_list(): conditionally emits annotations, provenance,
  and supportedZones in per-tool JSON
- connector_health/rate_limits/operations/global_status resources:
  use new inventory_truth_summary() for dynamic truth state instead
  of hardcoded status strings
- inventory_truth_summary(): derives aggregate source, availability,
  authoritative flag, and surface state from tool provenance
- sample_live_tool() test helper for provenance-aware tests

export_tools.rs:
- Added Deserialize derive to McpToolAnnotations to support serde
  round-trips with the new provenance infrastructure

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
